ocr.text: The data presents a demographic breakdown by age group and gender as well as various mechanisms for delivering financial
aid. In the age group of 35–44 years, there are 1 Female and 1 Male; in the 45–54 years age group, there are 1 Female and 15
Males; in the 55–64 years category, there are 6 Females and 28 Males; in the 65–74 years group, there are 6 Females and 55
Males; and finally, in the 75–84 years category, there is 1 Female and 1 Male.
Age Group  Females  Males
35–44 years  1  1
75–84 years  1  1
The mechanisms of financial aid include Output-Based Aid (OBA), which is defined as a results-based mechanism that is used
to deliver basic infrastructure and social services to the poor, and it is excluded for use predominantly outside of the health
sector. Cash on Delivery (COD) is defined as a mechanism that gives recipients full responsibility and authority over funds paid
in proportion to verified measures of progress toward a single specific outcome, but it is excluded due to political difficulty in
the agent having complete control. Performance-Based Financing (PBF) is defined as fee-for-service paid to providers
conditional on specific predefined indicators for degree and/or quality, and it is included for use in the health sector and political
feasibility.
Mechanism  Definition  Inclusion/Exclusion criteria
Output-Based Aid (OBA)  A results-based mechanism that is used to deliver basic infrastructure and social services to the poor  Excluded for use predominantly outside of the health sector

Cash on Delivery (COD)  A mechanism that gives recipients full responsibility and authority over funds paid in proportion to verified measures of progress toward a single specific outcome  Excluded for political difficulty in agent having complete control

Performance-Based Financing (PBF)  Fee-for-service paid to providers conditional on specific predefined indicators for degree and/or quality  Included for use in health sector and political feasibility

Performance-Based Contracting (PBC)  A fixed price for an output or outcome with a variable increase/decrease in payment based on performance, typically applied to NGOs  Included for use in health sector and political feasibility
Conditional Cash Transfers (CCTs)  A demand-side mechanism that provides incentives directly to program beneficiaries  Included for use in health sector and political feasibility
Performance-Based Contracting (PBC) is a fixed price for an output or outcome with a variable increase/decrease in payment
based on performance, included for use in health sector and political feasibility. Conditional Cash Transfers (CCTs) is defined
as a demand-side mechanism that provides incentives directly to program beneficiaries, also included for use in health sector
and political feasibility.
